name: Alpic MCP Server Manager
what_it_does: MCP server for managing, debugging, and analyzing other MCP servers deployed via Alpic. Enables AI agents to manage MCP server projects, monitor deployment status, and check analytics for MCP servers running in the Alpic platform — a management plane for MCP deployments.
use_cases: Managing and monitoring deployed MCP server projects from DevOps agents
Debugging MCP server issues and deployment problems from operations agents
Checking analytics and usage metrics for MCP servers from platform management agents
Automating MCP server deployment lifecycle management
not_for: MCP server development (deployment/management focus, not development)
Self-hosted MCP infrastructure without Alpic platform
best_when: A developer or team using Alpic to deploy MCP servers wants AI agents to help manage, monitor, and debug their MCP infrastructure through the Alpic management platform.
avoid_when: You don't deploy MCP servers on Alpic. This is a meta-MCP for managing other MCPs specifically within the Alpic ecosystem.

auth.notes: Alpic account and API credentials required. Management operations could affect deployed MCP servers — use appropriate access controls.

pricing.notes: Alpic is an emerging MCP deployment platform. Pricing not publicly documented.

known_agent_gotchas: AUTHORIZED USE ONLY: Management operations can affect live MCP deployments used by others
Meta-MCP concept: agents managing MCPs that other agents use creates circular dependency risks
Emerging platform — stability and feature set are not yet proven at production scale
Management credentials have elevated access — restrict to trusted agents only
Analytics data may include usage patterns of other users of your deployed MCPs
agent_readiness.security_notes: AUTHORIZED USE ONLY: Management plane access to MCP servers. Management credentials are high-privilege. Affects production deployments. Emerging platform with unproven security posture.

security_score_components.security_notes: Management plane access to live MCP deployments. High privilege credentials. Emerging platform. Restrict access carefully.
